D1_source_reachability.details: 9/9 URLs checked via real HEAD/GET (UA set, timeout=10s). OUP product page returned 202 Accepted for 7 series (S301-S305, S308, S309) — treated as reachable since the book PDF is offline-archival in Inputs/Capitalism Data/ (canonical primary). S306 and S307 cited https://archive.org/details/familyexpenditur0000alle which returned 404 (item not present). Confirmed via archive.org advancedsearch that Allen & Bowley (1935) is not on Internet Archive; the book is offline-archival in research libraries. Mark as offline-archival rather than failed; substitute extension URLs (ONS LCF) returned 200. Deduction: -3 (2 series with broken primary URLs requiring substitute documentation).

D2_year_coverage.details: 7/9 series are theoretical/simulation curves with no year dimension (year_range_book = [null,null]) — D2 inapplicable, scored full. S306/S307 are single-year (1904) cross-sections; coverage of the cited year is 100% conditional on obtaining Allen & Bowley (1935) Table 1 from a library. Deduction: -2 because the digitised tabulation has not yet been ingested into SalvagedInputs/ (HDARP flagged 'theoretical_no_data').

D3_license_compatibility.details: S301-S305, S308, S309: book figures (OUP, reproduction governed by OUP permissions for verbatim reuse; analytical regeneration from eqs 3.5-3.11 and stated parameters is unencumbered). S306-S307: UK Board of Trade 1904 enquiry is Crown Copyright (now public domain under expiry); Allen & Bowley 1935 monograph is past UK copyright term (>70 years p.m.a. for both Allen d.1983 — actually NOT yet PD for Allen, who died 1983; Bowley d.1957). Deduction: -1 because Allen's death (1983) means the 1935 monograph is still in copyright in UK until 2054; rely on the underlying Crown-Copyright tabulation directly via Cd. 3864 (1908) which IS public domain.

D4_units_consistency.details: All 9 series have explicit units fields in primary_source.units. Theoretical curves use model units (dimensionless income 0-60, dimensionless shares/expenditures); cross-sections use shillings/week for income and percent or shillings/week for food expenditure; NetLogo simulations use model-currency. No unit mismatches. Loaders for theoretical series will simulate to the same parameter ranges; loaders for S306/S307 must preserve shillings (no implicit £ conversion).

D5_extension_feasibility.details: S301-S305, S308, S309 (7 series): extension_status='not_applicable_theoretical' — analytical or simulation curves with no time dimension; no extension possible. S306, S307: extension_status='not_applicable_cross_sectional' — these are 1904 single-year cross-sections; the ONS LCF (modern UK Living Costs and Food Survey, https://www.ons.gov.uk/.../familyspendingintheuk/latest, HEAD 200) is a *separate* modern cross-section that can be published alongside as a comparator, NOT a temporal splice. Documented in both dossiers' extension_candidates with splice_strategy='none' and concerns about concept consistency (working-class urban 1904 vs full-population modern; shillings vs £). Deduction: -1 because the comparator publication decision (publish 1904-only vs 1904+modern paired cross-sections) is open for Phase 5.

D6_no_proxy_compliance.details: No proxy substitutions in this chapter. All 7 theoretical/simulation series are reproduced from Shaikh's own equations and stated parameters (no substitution possible — there is no 'original' to proxy). S306/S307 use the cited Allen & Bowley (1935) directly. Modern UK ONS LCF is NOT being used as a proxy for 1904 data; it would be published as a separate cross-section if Phase 5 elects to, with full Concept Match Justification per anu-framework rules.

per_series.name: Change in expenditure relative to change in income, Case I

content_type_correction.ratification_note: Phase 3 re-typing CONFIRMED. Fig 3.3 is an analytic curve from eqs (3.5)-(3.11) plotted over a synthetic income grid (y: 0-60 model units), not historical observations. HDARP linkage classifies as documentation_type='theoretical', is_empirical=false.

per_series.license: OUP book figure; analytical regeneration unencumbered
per_series.units: marginal expenditure share d(p1x1)/dy (dimensionless), plotted against income y
remediation: Phase 5: implement loader L301 as analytic generator from eqs (3.5)-(3.11); calibrate x1min(y) sub-linear path to match printed axis ranges.

per_series.license: OUP book figure; analytical regeneration unencumbered
per_series.units: expenditure share p1x1/y (dimensionless ratio, 0-1.2) plotted against income y (0-60)
remediation: Phase 5: implement loader L302 as analytic generator from eq (3.11); document parameter choices.

per_series.license: OUP book figure; analytical regeneration unencumbered

per_series.name: Engel Curve of Necessaries, Case II

per_series.license: OUP book figure; analytical regeneration unencumbered

per_series.name: Empirical Expenditure Share on Food (Working Class Budgets, United Kingdom, 1904)

content_type_correction.ratification_note: Phase 3 re-typing CONFIRMED. Fig 3.8 is a 1904 single-year cross-section of UK working-class households by income band, not a time series. Source: Allen & Bowley (1935), drawing on UK Board of Trade 1904 Cost-of-Living Enquiry (Cd. 3864, 1908).

per_series.license: Underlying UK Board of Trade 1904 enquiry: Crown Copyright (lapsed, public domain). Allen & Bowley 1935 monograph: still in UK copyright (Allen d.1983; expires 2054). Prefer citing Cd. 3864 (1908) directly to ensure PD status.
per_series.units: Share of household expenditure on food (percent of total weekly expenditure), 56%-70%; horizontal axis Average Income in shillings, 0-60

proposed_substitute.primary_source_substitute: Underlying primary: UK Board of Trade 1908, 'Report of an Enquiry by the Board of Trade into Working-Class Rents, Housing and Retail Prices, together with the Standard Rates of Wages prevailing in certain Occupations in the Principal Industrial Towns of the United Kingdom' (Cd. 3864). Available via HathiTrust full-view / British Library / partner research libraries (PARLIPAPERS subscription).
proposed_substitute.comparator_modern: UK ONS Living Costs and Food Survey, Family Spending in the UK (HEAD 200 https://www.ons.gov.uk/peoplepopulationandcommunity/personalandhouseholdfinances/expenditure/bulletins/familyspendingintheuk/latest). Publish as separate modern cross-section, NOT as a splice.
proposed_substitute.rationale: Allen-Bowley archive.org URL in dossier returned HTTP 404; advancedsearch confirms the 1935 monograph is not on Internet Archive. Original Cd. 3864 enquiry is the upstream Crown-Copyright tabulation Allen & Bowley re-presented and is preferable on both copyright and provenance grounds.

remediation: Phase 5 (loader L306): obtain Allen & Bowley (1935) Table 1 via library scan (HathiTrust or UK research library); transcribe (income, food-share) pairs; document via Concept Match Justification in EPR.
If Allen & Bowley unavailable, source the underlying tabulation from Cd. 3864 (1908) directly.
Update primary_source.url in S306_research.json once the working copy is located (replace dead archive.org URL).

per_series.name: Empirical Engel Curve for Food (Working Class Budgets, United Kingdom, 1904)

content_type_correction.ratification_note: Phase 3 re-typing CONFIRMED. Fig 3.9 is the absolute-expenditure Engel curve companion to Fig 3.8; same 1904 cross-section, different axis (shillings/week food expenditure vs shillings/week income).

per_series.license: Same as S306. Prefer Cd. 3864 (1908) Crown Copyright PD source.
per_series.units: Expenditure on Food (shillings per week), 0-35; horizontal axis Average Income in shillings, 0-60

proposed_substitute.primary_source_substitute: UK Board of Trade 1908 Cd. 3864 (as for S306).
proposed_substitute.comparator_modern: UK ONS Family Spending workbook 1: detailed expenditure and trends (HEAD 200 https://www.ons.gov.uk/peoplepopulationandcommunity/personalandhouseholdfinances/expenditure/datasets/familyspendingworkbook1detailedexpenditureandtrends). Publish as separate modern cross-section; requires shillings→£ currency note, not a temporal splice.
proposed_substitute.rationale: Allen-Bowley archive.org URL returned HTTP 404; pivot to Cd. 3864 as upstream PD source.

remediation: Phase 5 (loader L307): same Allen & Bowley / Cd. 3864 transcription as S306; extract (income, food-expenditure-in-shillings) pairs.
Update primary_source.url in S307_research.json once located.

per_series.name: Necessary Good (x1) Demand Curves, Four Different Micro Foundations

content_type_correction.ratification_note: Phase 3 re-typing CONFIRMED. Fig 3.10 overlays one analytical curve (eq 3.5) and four NetLogo simulation outputs (Neoclassical Homogeneous, Neoclassical Heterogeneous, Whimsical/Becker, Imitate-Innovate/Dosi). Simulation output, not historical data. Construction='composite' is the correct compositional flag.

per_series.license: OUP book figure; NetLogo source code 'available on request' per footnote 21 (Amr Ragab acknowledged) — not in public archive as of 2026-05-18. Re-implementation from verbal specs is unencumbered.
per_series.units: p1 in [0.80, 1.60] vs x1 in [70, 110] (model-currency price; model-units quantity)
remediation: Phase 5 (loader L308): re-implement four NetLogo models from verbal specs (Shaikh 2016 pp. 96-99) using NetLogo 6.x or Python (mesa); parameters y=200, pi=0.50, x1min=10, p1=1, p2=2; price sweep p1 1.0->1.5 in 0.01 steps.
Document Monte-Carlo statistical reproduction (random seeds not stated in book); report curves with confidence bands over multiple seeds.
Optional: contact Amr Ragab (NSSR) to request original NetLogo .nlogo files; if obtained, archive in SalvagedInputs/.

per_series.name: Luxury Good (x2) Demand Curves, Four Different Micro Foundations

content_type_correction.ratification_note: Phase 3 re-typing CONFIRMED. Fig 3.11 is the luxury-good companion to Fig 3.10; same four NetLogo models, price sweep p2 2.0->3.0. Simulation output.

per_series.units: p2 in [1.50, 3.25] vs x2 in [30, 50] (model-currency price; model-units quantity)
remediation: Phase 5 (loader L309): paired with L308 — same NetLogo re-implementation produces both curves and Table 3.1 elasticities; price sweep p2 2.0->3.0.

chapter_level_recommendations: Ratify all 7 Phase 3 content-type corrections (S301: time_series->theoretical; S302: cross_sectional->theoretical; S303-S305: time_series->theoretical; S306-S307: time_series->cross_sectional; S308-S309: time_series->theoretical). All 7 align with HDARP_SERIES_LINKAGE.json and the analytical/empirical structure of Ch 3.

Update primary_source.url for S306 and S307: the existing https://archive.org/details/familyexpenditur0000alle returns HTTP 404 and the item is not on Internet Archive (confirmed by archive.org advancedsearch). Replace with citation to UK Board of Trade 1908 Cd. 3864 (HathiTrust full-view / British Library); keep Allen & Bowley (1935) as the secondary citation Shaikh used.
Add `adequacy` block to all 9 series in series_registry.json with status='ready_for_phase5' and the per-series score contribution.

Phase 5 Stage decision: for S306/S307, decide whether to publish a single 1904 cross-section (faithful to Shaikh) or a paired (1904, modern ONS LCF) figure as a separate panel. The modern LCF is NOT a splice and NOT a proxy; if included it must be flagged as a comparator cross-section with concept-match justification (working-class urban 1904 vs full-population UK modern; shillings vs GBP).
open_questions: Should the NetLogo source code (S308/S309) be requested from Amr Ragab before Phase 5 starts, or is re-implementation from the published verbal specs acceptable? Recommend: re-implement (the verbal specs in Shaikh 2016 pp. 96-99 and Table 3.1 parameters are complete enough to reproduce the statistical pattern; Shaikh's central claim is that micro foundations don't matter, so reproducing the qualitative result is sufficient).

For S306/S307: should the loader transcribe directly from Allen & Bowley (1935) Table 1 (Shaikh's stated source, but copyright-encumbered until 2054 and not on Internet Archive), or pivot to the upstream UK Board of Trade Cd. 3864 (1908) tabulation (public domain, archival but harder to source)? Recommend Cd. 3864 with Allen & Bowley as cross-check.
